summary: Iteration 3 backend testing complete: parent mode (role-based register, generate-code, link/unlink, children/child views), scheduler startup, and mock email service. All 20 new tests in test_iteration3.py PASSED. Iter2 regression suite (20 tests) also PASSED. Backend logs confirm '✅ Background scheduler started (weekly reset Mon 00:00 Bucharest, reminders every 5min)' on startup.
critical_code_review_comments: POST /api/auth/register validates role against ('student','parent') and falls back to 'student' for invalid values - good defensive default.
Parent linking is correctly two-way: parent.children += child_id, child.parents += parent_id, and child.parent_code is invalidated (set None) preventing code reuse.

POST /api/parent/link with same code twice: behaviour verified - after first link the code is invalidated, so a duplicate-link test requires regenerating. Once the same parent calls link again on a new code, the API returns {already_linked: true, child_id}. Logic is correct but parents could repeatedly trigger code regeneration on the child - acceptable.

POST /api/parent/unlink/{child_id} does not verify the child_id actually exists in parent.children before pulling - it always returns ok:true. Minor: could return 404/400 when not linked, but current behaviour is idempotent and safe.
send_email is a pure mock returning True and logging only - clearly flagged as MOCKED. Replacement guidance is in module docstring.
Scheduler uses pytz Europe/Bucharest and APScheduler CronTrigger correctly; reminders run every 5 min via IntervalTrigger and mark sessions with reminder_sent=true to avoid duplicate emails.
server.py file is 822 lines and approaches the 700-line guideline; consider splitting routes into modules (auth, parent, payments, sessions) in a future iteration.
Mongo _id is consistently excluded across endpoints (projection {_id:0}); user_public also strips password_hash.
parent_link uses payload.code.upper().strip() for normalization - good UX.
test_credentials: Dynamic test users created via /api/auth/register (emails prefixed test.iter3.*)
seed_data_creation: None - dynamic users per test only. No persistent cleanup required as docs use uuid-suffixed emails.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 3 parent mode endpoints all green. Scheduler confirmed running. send_email is a MOCKED implementation that only logs - real provider integration is still pending. To verify weekly_reset/session_reminders execution end-to-end would require either manually invoking the coroutines or fast-forwarding time (not done in this run; only module-level imports + log verification were performed).
rca of the issue: No issues found in iteration 3 backend implementation.
